i) Sphenopsida A) Dryopteris
ii) Lycopsida B) Selaginella
iii) Psilopsida C) Psilotum
iv) Pteropsida D) Equisetum
A) A-iv,B-ii, C—iii, D-1i
B) A-iii, B-ii, C-iv,D-1i
C) A—ii, B—iii, C—i, D—iv
D) A-i, B-iv, C—ii, D — iii
70. Identify following pteridophytes — {(Pg37, E)
[4] i
(&] :
(D)
A) A = Salvinia, B = horsetail, C = fern, D
= Selaginella
B) A = Selaginella, B = Salvinia, C = fern,
D = horsetail
C) A = Equisteum, B = fern, C =
Selaginella, D = horsetail
D) A = Selaginella, B = Salvia, C =
Dryopteris, D = Equisteum
71. Label A, B, C, D, E in following diagram:
(Pg37, E)
w
(8
©)
(0)
1K)
A) A = strobilus, B = rhizome,
B) A = cone, C = Node, D = internode
C) A = strobilus, B = rhizome, C = node, D
= internode, E = branch
D) None of these
Paragraph - 3.4
Gymnosperm:
72. Gymnosperms are plants in which —
